sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Villarreal, currently sitting at 5th in the league with 55 points, host Osasuna, who are 8th with 44 points, in a crucial Round 34 clash on May 3, 2025, at 14:15. The game at Estadio de la Ceramica in Villarreal promises to be an intriguing battle between two sides with contrasting ambitions. Villarreal will look to solidify their top-half position, while Osasuna aim to close the gap and push for a higher finish.

sections.subtitle: Historical Context
sections.text: Looking at the last 5 matchups between these teams, Villarreal have dominated with 3 wins and 2 draws, leaving Osasuna without a victory. Their most recent encounter on November 24, 2024, ended in a thrilling 2-2 draw, with Osasuna’s Ante Budimir earning Player of the Match honors with an impressive Sofascore rating of 8.7. Head-to-head streaks show Villarreal unbeaten in their last 5 against Osasuna, though neither team has kept a clean sheet in their last 3 meetings.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Osasuna come into this match in strong form, with 3 consecutive wins and an unbeaten run of 5 games. They’ve also been the first to score in 4 of their last 5 matches, showing early aggression. Villarreal, on the other hand, have seen over 2.5 goals in 5 of their last 7 games, indicating a tendency for high-scoring encounters. However, their streak of less than 4.5 cards in 4 of their last 5 games suggests a disciplined approach recently.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Villarreal
sections.text: Villarreal boast several standout players, including Alejandro Baena, who ranks 1st in the league for key passes with 87 and 3rd for big chances created with 17, alongside 7 assists (4th in the league). Ayoze Pérez has been lethal upfront, scoring 14 goals against an expected goals tally of 10.43, overperforming by 3.57. Gerard Moreno also impresses with an 80% aerial duel win rate, ranking 2nd in the league, while defender Sergi Cardona matches Baena with 7 assists, placing him 4th league-wide.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Osasuna
sections.text: Osasuna’s attack is spearheaded by Ante Budimir, who has netted 18 goals this season, ranking 3rd in the league, overperforming his expected goals of 16.63 by 1.37. Rubén García has been in fine form over the last 5 matches, scoring 2 goals and providing 1 assist with a Sofascore rating of 7.5. Youngster Aimar Oroz adds creativity in midfield with 4 goals and 2 assists, while goalkeeper Aitor Fernández has been exceptional recently, averaging an 8.4 Sofascore rating over the last 5 games.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Villarreal’s attacking prowess is evident with 56 goals scored this season, ranking 3rd in the league, and 96 big chances created, also 3rd. They excel in headers (10 goals, 3rd) and accurate crosses (180, 2nd), which could trouble Osasuna’s defense. Osasuna, however, are dangerous late in games, with 26.09% of their goals coming between the 76th and 90th minutes. Villarreal’s high penalty area entries (29.09 per game, 5th) suggest they’ll push for dominance in Osasuna’s box.

sections.subtitle: Missing Players Impact
sections.text: Villarreal will miss midfielder Yéremy Pino, who has 3 goals and 5 assists this season, alongside defender Raúl Albiol, which could weaken their backline. Osasuna are without key midfielder Jon Moncayola and defender Alejandro Catena, whose 9% long ball rate ranks 2nd in the league, potentially impacting their distribution from the back. These absences might tilt the balance slightly in favor of Villarreal’s depth at home.

sections.subtitle: Disciplinary and Set-Piece Expectations
sections.text: Referee Alejandro Quintero Gonzalez averages 5.2 yellow cards per game and 0.28 red cards, indicating a potentially fiery encounter, especially with both teams averaging over 4.5 cards in 4 of their last 5 head-to-heads. Villarreal average 5.12 corners per game (6th in the league), while Osasuna have seen under 10.5 corners in 8 of their last 10 matches and 4 of 5 head-to-heads, suggesting a moderate set-piece battle.
